subject: [CLAMP] Release 5.1.4
commit_message:
[CLAMP] Release 5.1.4

Do the release 5.1.4 + fix potential issue with memory taken by clamp backend.
Heap is now dynamic so resources must be set by default, onap/values overwrites the current flavor to unlimited.

Issue-ID: CLAMP-956

Signed-off-by: sebdet <sebastien.determe@intl.att.com>
Change-Id: I349b9454ca0d7a3f2724aa27c041a4ea2da3689f
Signed-off-by: Sylvain Desbureaux <sylvain.desbureaux@orange.com>

Patch Set 6:

I’m a bit concerned by setting values even in « unlimited »...
We could also « force » static values when flavor is unlimited no?

Let’s have a discussion all together in order to find the best way to do that!

Patch Set 6:

Yeah I agree, a better solution shoud be found. In fact, unlimited should not exist for Clamp backend container as the memory size is now proportional to the ram size. If it's not set, clamp is going to consume your node memory.

Patch Set 6:

we could also imagine this for clamp backend:

* if set to unlimited, then we set "-Xms256m -Xmx1g" as ram option
* if not, then we set "-XX:MinRAMPercentage=50 -XX:MaxRAMPercentage=75" as ram option

what do you (and the other guys) think?

Patch Set 6:

Sylvain, yes we could do that, sounds reasonable.
Uploaded patch set 7.

Patch Set 8:

hey guys,
maybe a good deal when on "limited" pods would be to set MaxRAM to 85/90%?
And I think we would all be happy no?

Patch Set 8:

> Patch Set 8:
> 
> hey guys,
> maybe a good deal when on "limited" pods would be to set MaxRAM to 85/90%?
> And I think we would all be happy no?

We can try with 85%, but as I said we need to keep a security, otherwise we may have issues

Patch Set 8: Code-Review+1

> Patch Set 8:
> 
> > Patch Set 8:
> > 
> > hey guys,
> > maybe a good deal when on "limited" pods would be to set MaxRAM to 85/90%?
> > And I think we would all be happy no?
> 
> We can try with 85%, but as I said we need to keep a security, otherwise we may have issues

No reason to do that. All shame on me...
I just ramped up my knowledge around this and 75% is actually the recommended value in many sources

https://dev.to/focusedlabs/the-no-nonsense-guide-to-jvm-14-memory-on-kubernetes-508m
